comments: The identification of menthol as a TRPV5 inhibitor has therapeutic potential, but the slow-blocking kinetics and selectivity over other TRP channels warrant further characterization.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Menthol inhibits TRPV5 through direct channel blockade
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: menthol acts as slow blocker and binds to W583 in the permeation pathway

title: Proton channels govern vesicular carbonate chemistry in mineralizing cells of a marine calcifier
task: Elucidate proton channel control of pH and carbonate chemistry in calcifying vesicles during biomineralization
key_results: Otop2l proton channel regulates vesicular pH and membrane proton conductance in sea urchin calcifying cells; Otop2l activated by alkaline pH and Mg2+/Ca2+ ions; alkaline intracellular conditions drive amorphous calcium carbonate (ACC) precursor formation
comments: The identification of Otop2l as a pH regulator in biomineralization provides mechanistic insight into calcifier vulnerability to ocean acidification, though the relative contributions of Otop2l versus other proton transporters remain to be quantified.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Proton channels control pH in calcifying vesicles to favor mineral precursor formation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: Otop2l generates high intracellular proton conductance enabling alkaline conditions in ACC-forming endocytotic vesicles

hypotheses.hypothesis: Otop2l activation depends on ions and pH present in calcifying cells
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: Otop2l activated by alkaline pH, Mg2+, and Ca2+ ions

title: Magnetic resonance control of spin-correlated radical pair dynamics in vivo
task: Demonstrate in vivo magnetic resonance control of spin-correlated radical pair dynamics
key_results: Radiofrequency magnetic fields near electron spin resonance frequency modify red fluorescent protein (mScarlet) emission in presence of flavin cofactor in C. elegans at room temperature; indicates radical pair coherence time >4 ns in vivo
comments: This work represents a notable proof-of-concept for quantum effects in living organisms, though the practical utility for gene expression control and the coherence mechanisms require further validation.

inputs: radiofrequency magnetic field at ESR frequency

hypotheses.hypothesis: Magnetic fields can control spin-correlated radical pair reactions in vivo
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: radiofrequency fields modulate RFP-flavin fluorescence in live C. elegans with coherence time >4 ns

title: In vivo site-specific engineering to reprogram T cells

key_results: Therapeutic levels of CAR T cells generated in vivo in humanized mouse models of B cell aplasia, hematological and solid malignancies with stable, cell-specific transgene expression
comments: Addresses major accessibility bottleneck by eliminating ex vivo manufacturing, though translation to clinical efficacy and off-target integration rates require further validation.

hypotheses.result: Confirmed: therapeutic-level CAR T cells generated in vivo with stable expression in humanized mouse tumor models

hypotheses.hypothesis: T cell-specific delivery optimization of both vectors improves targeting efficiency and reduces off-target effects
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: optimized vectors achieved CAR generation in vivo across multiple tumor models

title: Caspase-8 comes in from the CYLD

title: Repression of EGFR by new biguanide 4C potentiated ovarian cancer to PARP inhibitors through down-regulation of BRCA2 and Rad51

title: Mitochondrial DNA release via VDAC1 in keratinocytes: a key driver of innate immunity and vitiligo pathogenesis

comments: Reveals how dissipation-driven boundary phenomena intertwine with topological protection, offering dynamical probes of spectral and band topology.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Skin effect and topological edge states produce independent damping processes with separable timescales
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; boundary-induced spectral topology decouples timescales

title: Injection locking of Rydberg dissipative time crystals
task: Injection locking of Rydberg dissipative time crystals
key_results: Full synchronization achieved above critical RF amplitude threshold; locking bandwidth scales linearly with RF amplitude; harmonics entrained via nonlinear temporal dynamics
comments: Demonstrates practical control of time-crystalline order in strongly interacting quantum media, bridging classical nonlinear dynamics and quantum many-body physics.

model_io.model: Rydberg gas DTC under RF injection

hypotheses.hypothesis: RF field can synchronize intrinsic limit-cycle oscillations via injection locking
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; synchronization occurs with linear bandwidth scaling

hypotheses.hypothesis: Higher-order harmonics can be entrained in nonlinear quantum medium
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; harmonic synchronization observed

title: Unusual diffusion of chiral active Brownian particles in deformable and displaceable media
task: Diffusion of chiral active Brownian particles in deformable media
key_results: Nonmonotonic diffusivity vs obstacle density with ~100× enhancement; competition between collimation and velocity suppression; enhancement nonmonotonic in orbital radius
comments: Competition between opposing mechanisms produces counterintuitive nonmonotonic transport, relevant for active matter control in reconfigurable environments.

model_io.model: Chiral active Brownian particles in annular channel with deformable rings

hypotheses.hypothesis: Obstacle-induced collimation enhances diffusion at low densities; suppression dominates at high densities
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; nonmonotonic peak observed

title: Collapse of Jahn-Teller phonons in La1−xSrxMnO3 with weak magnetoresistance
task: Jahn-Teller phonon collapse and electron-phonon coupling in manganites La₁₋ₓSrₓMnO₃
key_results: Jahn-Teller optical phonons collapse above Curie temperature despite weak magnetoresistance; neutrons confirm ferromagnetic phase conventional; giant EPC in charge/orbital channel; magnetoresistance correlates with oxygen sublattice diffusion rate, not EPC strength
comments: Decouples colossal magnetoresistance mechanism from Jahn-Teller strength, implicating diffusive carrier trapping as primary driver—fundamentally revising CMR paradigm.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Jahn-Teller EPC strength does not directly control magnetoresistance magnitude; diffusion rate of charge-trapping oxygen distortions does
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; anomalous phonon collapse despite weak CMR

title: Mushroom biotech startups help address global challenges
task: Survey translational pathways from mushroom biotechnology research to commercial products
key_results: Identified mushroom biotech startups as vehicles for advancing mycology, biofabrication, and bioinspired innovation toward sustainable industry applications
comments: Perspective piece focused on ecosystem analysis rather than mechanistic investigation; lacks quantitative benchmarks for impact assessment.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Mushroom-based biotechnology represents a cross-sectoral model for translating bioinspired innovation
hypotheses.result: Startup ecosystem analysis demonstrates significant impact on sustainability and human wellbeing

title: Large-scale multi-omics profiling reveals environmental and evolutionary drivers of fungal phylogeographic and metabolic diversity
task: Identify environmental and evolutionary drivers of fungal metabolic diversity across populations
key_results: Phylogeographic analysis of 1052 A. flavus strains revealed population-specific mycotoxin production; metabolome-genome-wide association and genotype-environment association identified regulation and primary metabolism as primary drivers (not biosynthetic gene variation alone) of specialized metabolite differentiation
comments: Large-scale multi-omics integration is comprehensive, but mechanistic links between environmental shifts and metabolic rewiring require deeper transcriptional/regulatory analysis beyond pan-transcriptome studies.

model_io.model: Metabolites-genome-wide association study (mGWAS) and genotype-environment association (GEA)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Biosynthetic gene cluster variation is the primary driver of metabolic differentiation across fungal populations
hypotheses.result: Variation in pathway genes only partially explains metabolic profiles; regulation and primary metabolism are major contributors

hypotheses.hypothesis: Environmental shifts drive adaptive evolution of fungal metabolic profiles
hypotheses.result: Low-mycotoxin clades harbor distinct uncharacterized biosynthetic clusters and show strong phylogeographic metabolic patterns linked to ecological niches

title: Avoidance of rejuvenation: a stress test for evolutionary theories of aging
task: Resolve evolutionary paradox of rejuvenation avoidance under stress using lifespan plasticity models
key_results: Pathogen control hypothesis explains rejuvenation avoidance better than classical aging theories; mathematical model predicts evolution of genetic programs for adaptive aging mechanisms in eusocial insects
comments: Strong theoretical framework but relies on eusocial insect models; applicability to mammalian/human aging and rejuvenation feasibility remains speculative.

model_io.model: Mathematical model of lifespan plasticity in eusocial insects

hypotheses.hypothesis: Classic theories of aging explain avoidance of rejuvenation in eusocial insects
hypotheses.result: Standard formulations require substantial additional assumptions; poor explanatory power

hypotheses.hypothesis: Pathogen control hypothesis predicts evolved avoidance of rejuvenation as adaptive aging mechanism
hypotheses.result: Mathematical model shows consistent evolutionary framework for lifespan termination programs under pathogenic threat

title: Synthetic circuits for cell ratio control
task: Control population ratios of multiple cell types via recombinase-mediated genetic circuits
key_results: Achieved precise binary and multistep cell-fate differentiation from single founder strains; optimized yeast consortia for pigmentation and cellulose degradation with defined cell-type ratios
comments: Comprehensive experimental validation across prokaryotes and eukaryotes, though scalability to higher-order multi-cell-type systems remains to be demonstrated.

model_io.model: Recombinase-based branching circuits (parallel and series topologies)
Data-driven predictive model for binary differentiation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Recombinase efficiency and circuit topology determine achievable population ratios
hypotheses.result: Systematic characterization enabled reliable prediction and construction of precision fermentation consortia
